statement: Douglas George Townsend was on duty at the Adelaide Railway Station ticket box from 6:15 a.m. to about 2:00 p.m. on 30 November 1948.
uncertainty: Exact duty interval is sourced; ticket issue time is separate.

statement: The second-class Adelaide-to-Henley Beach railway ticket was issued on 30 November 1948, but exact issue time and purchaser identity are unknown.
uncertainty: Purchaser and issue time unresolved.

statement: The Adelaide-Somerton bus ticket associated with the body was issued for a route departing about 11:15 a.m.
uncertainty: Holder, boarding point, and alighting point unresolved.

statement: No witness came forward to say the man was seen at Somerton between the bus-arrival interval and 7:00 p.m.
uncertainty: Negative witness evidence is not proof of absence.

statement: At 7:00 p.m. a man was seen lying where the body was later found and raising his right arm.
uncertainty: Identity continuity not proven by facial identification.

statement: At about 7:30 p.m. a man and girl observed a man in the same place and saw no movement.
uncertainty: Does not establish with certainty that the person was the deceased.

statement: A medical estimate placed death around about 2:00 a.m.
uncertainty: Medical estimate, not observed time.

statement: The body of an unknown man was found on the shore at Somerton Beach at about 7:00 a.m. on 1 December 1948.
uncertainty: First-discovery witness details still require page-level confirmation.

statement: Dr Bennett examined the body at about 9:40 a.m. and estimated death about eight hours earlier.
uncertainty: Time of death estimate approximate.

statement: The initial clothing search found a railway ticket, bus ticket, cigarettes, combs, and chewing gum; the Tamam Shud slip was not found then.
uncertainty: Exact search time unresolved.

statement: The body was taken to the City Mortuary.
uncertainty: Exact transfer time unresolved.
notes: Used as early police sequence event.

statement: Cowan received stomach and contents, liver and muscle, urine, and blood on 2 December 1948.
uncertainty: Specimen chain beyond receipt not reconstructed here.

statement: Cowan was unable to find signs of any common poison in submitted specimens.
uncertainty: Not equivalent to proving absence of every possible toxic agent.

statement: The Tamam Shud slip was later found in a difficult-to-locate trouser fob pocket.
uncertainty: Exact date, time, and finder unresolved.

statement: The Tamam Shud paper was associated by the coroner with a second edition Fitzgerald Rubaiyat.
uncertainty: Book recovery chronology and provenance remain unresolved.

statement: An unclaimed suitcase was found at the Adelaide Railway Station luggage office a little over a month after 1 December 1948.
uncertainty: Exact date, inventory, and custody unresolved.

statement: The suitcase was assessed as internally connected by clothing size, removed tags, and thread similarity.
uncertainty: Does not establish ownership or complete custody.

statement: DOC-0001 documents 1949 inquest proceedings and remarks concerning the Somerton death.
uncertainty: Page-level witness testimony indexed separately.

statement: The inquest was adjourned sine die after recording the deceased as unknown, death as not natural, and precise circumstances and cause as unable to be stated with certainty.
uncertainty: Not converted into a modern cause-of-death conclusion.

statement: A later file is identified as GRG 1/27 File 53/1958 dated 14 March 1958.
uncertainty: Content review and original-custodian access remain limited.

statement: A certified copy of the unknown man's death certificate was issued by the Registrar on 14 May 2014.
uncertainty: Documentation event, not original death event.

statement: The preserved 1949 inquest reproduction includes an administrative return/date-stamp sequence before digitization.
uncertainty: Administrative handling event only.

statement: The 2021 exhumation is retained only as an unable-to-verify later placeholder in the primary-source chronology.
uncertainty: Official primary release not acquired and reviewed for Mission 4.
notes: Not treated as verified chronology here.

statement: The 2022-2026 Webb identity and genetic-genealogy chronology is retained only as an unable-to-verify later placeholder in this chronology layer.
uncertainty: No acquired official DNA, coroner, SAPOL, or Forensic Science SA report closes identity in the reviewed corpus.

statement: Eight unsupported or unable-to-verify timeline claims were removed or logged as chronology uncertainties rather than admitted as events.
uncertainty: Includes unsupported leads such as Woomera/Maralinga/Kaldor/train-ticket claims in Mission 5 summary.
